nodes.title: Return an inner function that keeps state
nodes.why: The returned function closes over `n`, so each counter instance has its own private state.
nodes.alternative: Use a global variable for the counter value.
nodes.tradeoff: Encapsulated and safe per instance, with closure syntax that beginners may need to learn.

nodes.title: Capture current value before incrementing
nodes.why: This ensures each call returns the current number, then advances for the next call.
nodes.tradeoff: Matches expected behavior exactly, with one extra local variable.

nodes.title: Return the captured value
nodes.why: The function output stays deterministic and easy to reason about across calls.
nodes.alternative: Return the updated value after increment.
nodes.tradeoff: Predictable call sequence, but different semantics from pre-increment style counters.
